response.justification: While James Smith has tangential geospatial exposure through the GISCollective open-source project and limited historical C++ usage at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis, his primary expertise for the past decade is TypeScript, Node.js, and web development. The role demands modern C++ or Rust as a core competency for production routing data pipelines; C++ does not even appear in his skills section. His geospatial work is an NGO-grade side project rather than production routing tile infrastructure, and there is no evidence of navigation systems, map pipeline engineering, or systems-level C++ in any recent role.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform, GIS mapping tools, FOSS4G speaker)
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, high-performance databases)
Broad infrastructure comfort including Linux, Kubernetes, microservices, and CI/CD — useful supporting skills for a pipeline role
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years stale (National Instruments 2014–2015, bachelor thesis); no Rust experience anywhere in the resume
All recent and current roles are TypeScript/Node.js/web-focused — no systems-level or data pipeline engineering in active work
No routing tile, navigation guidance, or production map data pipeline experience; geospatial work is hobbyist/NGO scale, not routing infrastructure

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
